2606.11189.title: A Unifying Lens on Supervised Fine-Tuning Through Target Distribution Design
2606.11189.paper_summary: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) typically maximizes the likelihood of every token in a demonstrated trajectory. However, an observed token can be non-unique, noisy, or misaligned with the model prior. Strictly fitting toward this one-hot target may be suboptimal, especially when the pretrained model encodes a rich knowledge prior. In this work, we reinterpret SFT as target distribution design: instead of studying only the loss objective, we analyze the token-level target that the loss drives the model to match. We introduce the Q-target framework, which decomposes SFT supervision into two explicit choices: (1) how strongly to rely on the observed token, and (2) how to allocate the remaining probability mass over alternatives. This perspective unifies many existing SFT variants as implicit choices of the target distribution Q. Building on this view, we propose Target-SFT which constructs the training objective directly from the desired target distribution. This method consistently outperforms across the ten reasoning dataset-model settings evaluated, showing the effectiveness of this target-based approach. Overall, our formulation reveals a more fundamental design principle for SFT training and opens a broader search space for SFT objectives.
2606.11189.paper_summary_zh: 監督微調 (SFT) 通常會最大化每個在示範軌跡中的標記的可能性。<lb/>然而，觀察到的標記可能是非唯一的、嘈雜的，或與模型的先驗不一致。<lb/>嚴格地朝著這個單一目標進行擬合可能不是最佳的，特別是當預訓練模型編碼了豐富的知識先驗時。<lb/>在本研究中，我們將 SFT 重新詮釋為目標分佈設計：我們不僅研究損失目標，還分析損失驅動模型匹配的標記級目標。<lb/>我們引入了 Q-target 框架，將 SFT 監督分解為兩個明確的選擇：(1) 依賴觀察到的標記的強度，和 (2) 如何將剩餘的概率質量分配給其他選擇。<lb/>這種觀點統一了許多現有的 SFT 變體，作為目標分佈 Q 的隱含選擇。<lb/>基於這一觀點，我們提出了 Target-SFT，該方法直接從所需的目標分佈構建訓練目標。<lb/>這種方法在評估的十個推理數據集-模型設置中始終表現優於其他方法，顯示了這種基於目標的方法的有效性。<lb/>總體而言，我們的公式揭示了 SFT 訓練的一個更基本的設計原則，並為 SFT 目標開辟了更廣泛的搜索空間。
2606.11189.author: Tong Xie et.al.
2606.11189.authors: Tong Xie, Yuanhao Ban, Yunqi Hong, Sohyun An, Yihang Chen, Cho-Jui Hsieh
